design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate alternating side-scrolling and overhead perspective sections while shooting enemies and projectiles; defeat mini-bosses at section ends and super-villains at stage conclusions to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Constant forward scroll pressure combined with dense enemy waves and projectile avoidance; loss of all upgrades on death creates severe punishment for mistakes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command the cosmic power of the Silver Surfer, growing stronger through collected upgrades (options, firepower, bombs) while engaging Marvel villains in escalating cosmic battles
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Embraces punishing difficulty as core identity ('Nintendo hard' exemplar); resets all upgrades on death; no difficulty settings; forces mastery through repetition and pattern memorization; allows level select and password progression for motivated players willing to engage with its brutality

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives; loss of all spheres (options), firepower upgrades, and bombs upon death; must re-collect from enemies
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resuming from completed stages with selectable upgrades, providing a meta-progression ladder for persevering players
difficulty.checkpointing: Stages divided into sections with mini-boss encounters; only the third section contains super-villain; no mid-section checkpoints within scrolling sequences
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through password system enabling selective upgrades (invincibility, enhanced weapons); level select allows tackling stages in any order (similar to Mega Man); no difficulty slider but progression through grinding and pattern mastery is always available

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible silver spheres ('options') that shoot additional pellets (repositionable to fire forward/sideways/backward); 'F' squares incrementally enhance firepower up to 5x; 'B' squares stock screen-clearing bombs; upgrades are permanently lost on death but password system allows re-enabling them
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level select after initial playthrough; two additional 'quests' unlock via passwords earned from mission completion; Galactus sends player to Magik Domain finale after first level set complete
replayability_hooks: Multiple quest modes unlocked through password progression
Level select allows different stage orders and tactical approaches
Password system encourages optimization runs with pre-selected upgrades
Marvel villain roster (Reptyl, Mephisto, Possessor, Fire Lord, Kylor) and final boss encounter structure rewards mastery

world_building_techniques: Marvel Comics character roster (Galactus, Mephisto, X-Men villain Mister Sinister) establishes cosmic scope
Narrative framing: Galactus tasks Silver Surfer with retrieving 'Cosmic Device' to prevent wrong-handed access
Stage progression tied to villain encounters from source material

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating threat structure: mini-bosses appear at section ends (only time screen stops scrolling), super-villains conclude stages with significantly higher durability. Final boss is mysterious giant purple entity with handgun (possibly Mister Sinister proxy). Boss encounters interrupt scrolling rhythm, forcing different tactical engagement than standard level navigation
notable_bosses: Final Cosmic Device Guardian (purple being, unclear identity, possibly Mister Sinister)

faction_indicators: Galactus represents cosmic authority delegating missions
X-Men universe connection (Mister Sinister as potential final antagonist)
danger_signals: Cosmic Device as MacGuffin representing existential threat if obtained by wrong faction
Mephisto and Possessor (mind-control/demonic entities) signal supernatural danger
Galactus's mission structure implies universal-scale stakes
environmental_storytelling: Magik Domain as final quest location signals darkened/corrupted space
Progressive villain encounters establish escalating cosmic threat hierarchy
Screen-pause during boss encounters emphasizes confrontation significance vs. continuous scroll pressure
